title: Tree Search Visualization of Uninformed Strategies
description: A side-by-side comparison of how Breadth-First Search (BFS), Depth-First Search (DFS), and Uniform-Cost Search (UCS) explore a state space tree from a start node (S) to a goal node (G). The frontier (nodes to be explored) is shown for each step, highlighting the different order of node expansion.
relationships: BFS expands all nodes at depth d before moving to d+1 (queue FIFO order)
DFS expands the deepest node first, backtracking when necessary (stack LIFO order)
UCS expands the node with the lowest cumulative path cost from S (priority queue order)
